_meta.author: Claude (national autopilot, VA batch)
_meta.note: VA state-tier evidence finalization for 5 records: 2 House R Delegates (Tata HD-99, Bloxom Jr HD-100) and 3 newly-sworn statewide Ds (Gov. Spanberger, Lt Gov. Hashmi, AG Jones). Bloxom flagged: YES on HJ9 marriage amendment (named GOP crossover) + publicly opposed school vouchers (second cross-party deviation). Jones: 2025 verbatim text-scandal quotes documented. Spanberger: 6-yr US House record + Jan-May 2026 gubernatorial signings.

profile.religion: Christian — works in Christian media (K-LOVE/Air1, TBN, CBN); Moravian background reported
profile.background: VA House of Delegates HD-99 since Jan 2024 (HD-82 Jan 2022–Jan 2024). Virginia Beach. Former medical-sales rep & political fundraiser; writer/host for K-LOVE/Air1 (EMF Broadcasting), TBN's 'Joy In Our Town,' CBN contributor. Author of novel 'Of Great Worth.' Daughter of an Army chaplain; father-in-law was Del. Bob Tata (HD-85).

anne-ferrell-tata.notes_set: VA House Delegate, HD-99 (R), since Jan 2024 (prior HD-82 Jan 2022–Jan 2024). [2026-06 overnight refinement, state tier] Uniformly conservative voting record where individually cited: NAY on the 2025 reproductive-freedom constitutional amendment (HJ1, party-line 51-48); chief-patron of 20-week abortion ban (HB1274) and born-alive protection (HB1795/HB304); rated 'Anti-Choice Extremist' by Choice Tracker. NOT among the 7 named GOP crossovers (or 5 abstainers) on HJ9 same-sex-marriage amendment — held the conservative line. YEA on the 2022 CRT ban (HB787) and 2022 religious-freedom bill (HB775). YEA on 2025 'Defend the Guard' (HB2193). NAY on SB69 (2024 DACA-eligibility expansion for law enforcement). Re-elected Nov 2025 over Cat Porterfield 54-46. Freedom Index 67% in 2024-25. No cross-party deviations found.

0.note: NAY on HJ1 2025 reproductive-freedom amendment; chief-patroned 20-week ban (HB1274) + born-alive bills (HB1795/HB304); campaign: 'Anne Ferrell is pro-life and believes that the unborn deserve our protection.'

NAY on HJ9 (2025) same-sex marriage constitutional amendment — not among the 7 named GOP crossovers (Bloxom, Coyner, Garrett, Green, McNamara, Obenshain, Wachsmann) or 5 abstainers; held conservative line.

1.note: Opposed state codification of same-sex marriage.

2.note: YEA on 2022 HB787 (Critical Race Theory ban in K-12).

1.note: YEA on 2022 HB775 (Religious Freedom protections).

0.note: YEA on 2025 HB2193 'Defend the Guard' (10A / anti-commandeering — restricts VA National Guard deployment absent congressional war declaration).

profile.religion: United Methodist — active member, Grace United Methodist Church
profile.background: VA House of Delegates HD-100 (Eastern Shore — Accomack/Northampton + part of Virginia Beach) since Feb 2014. Owner Shore Tire & Auto and two NAPA stores in Onley/Mappsville plus Egret Pointe Seafood Co. Son of Del./VA Sec. of Agriculture Robert 'Bob' Bloxom Sr. (HD-100, 1978–2004).

robert-bloxom-jr.notes_set: VA House Delegate, HD-100 (R), since Feb 2014 — 12 years; rural Eastern Shore moderate-conservative. [2026-06 overnight refinement, state tier] FLAGGED CROSS-PARTY DEVIATIONS: (1) Voted YES on HJ9 (2025) and HJ3 (2026 re-passage) — same-sex-marriage constitutional amendment — one of only 7 named GOP crossovers in 2025 (Bloxom, Coyner, Garrett, Green, McNamara, Obenshain, Wachsmann); forum statement: 'supports protecting same-sex marriage.' (2) Publicly opposed a school voucher program at the 2025 ESVA Chamber forum. Held conservative line elsewhere where individually cited: pro-life ('Rob is pro-life,' forum quote that HJ1 amendment 'would enshrine partial birth abortions into law'); YEA on 2025 'Defend the Guard'; YEA on 2022 CRT ban + family-life-education transparency; FOP Lodge 8 endorsement; pro-2A campaign platform (Castle Doctrine, opposes 'any national gun registry'); NRA-PVF endorsed. Freedom Index lifetime 29% — moderate by GOP caucus standard. Won re-election Nov 4, 2025 over Liz Richardson 55-45.

0.note: Campaign: 'Rob is pro-life'; 2025 ESVA Chamber forum quote that HJ1 'would enshrine partial birth abortions into law.'

CROSS-PARTY DEVIATION: voted YES on HJ9 (2025 same-sex-marriage constitutional amendment) — one of 7 named GOP crossovers (Bloxom, Coyner, Garrett, Green, McNamara, Obenshain, Wachsmann); voted YES again on HJ3 2026 re-passage; forum: 'supports protecting same-sex marriage.'

1.note: Same HJ9 YES vote codifies same-sex marriage in the state constitution.

0.note: CROSS-PARTY DEVIATION: at 2025 ESVA Chamber candidate forum publicly opposed a school voucher program — against school choice. (Also VEA 'Legislative Rookie of the Year' 2015 — teachers'-union alignment.)

2.note: YEA on 'Critical Race Theory ban' and 'family-life education transparency' votes per Freedom Index.

0.note: Endorsed by Virginia Beach FOP Lodge 8 — affirmative police-association endorsement of his record.
YEA on 'Defend the Guard' (10A / anti-commandeering — restricts VA National Guard deployment absent congressional war declaration).

1.note: Campaign: 'believes in our constitutional right to keep and bear arms,' opposes 'any national gun registry'; NRA-PVF endorsement listed; Commonwealth Sportsmen's Alliance contribution 2024.

3.note: Campaign site explicitly supports the Castle Doctrine.

profile.religion: Protestant (unspecified) — no specific congregation publicly stated
profile.background: 75th Governor of Virginia (first woman); sworn in Jan 17, 2026. Former US Rep VA-07 (2019–2025, 3 terms; did not seek reelection 2024). Prior career: CIA case officer (counterterrorism); US Postal Inspector. Blue Dog Coalition + New Democrat + Problem Solvers Caucus member in the House.

abigail-spanberger.notes_set: Governor of Virginia (D), sworn Jan 17, 2026 (75th Gov, first woman); won 2025 race ~57% over Lt. Gov. Earle-Sears. [2026-06 overnight refinement, state tier] Progressive on social/cultural issues with centrist-Dem posture on policing and a Blue Dog fiscal record. Heritage Action 5%. Pro-abortion: cosponsored + voted YES (twice) on the federal Women's Health Protection Act; signed SB449 Feb 6 2026 sending VA reproductive-freedom constitutional amendment to Nov 2026 ballot; signed contraceptive-access bills April 2026; endorsed by Planned Parenthood, EMILYs List, Reproductive Freedom for All. Pro-same-sex-marriage: voted YES on Respect for Marriage Act 2022 (twice); signed HB612 marriage-amendment ballot referral; original cosponsor Equality Act; HRC endorsement. Anti-school-choice: campaign platform 'oppose efforts that take public dollars out of public schools'; 'get culture wars out of our schools.' Pro-gun-control: cosponsored + voted YES on Assault Weapons Ban 2022 (HR1808); signed VA SB749/HB217 assault-weapons ban May 14–15 2026. Pro-mass-mail-in voting / opposes SAVE Act citizenship verification. Anti-sanctuary-cooperation: Executive Directive 1 (Feb 4 2026) terminated all VA 287(g) agreements (State Police, DOC, Wildlife Resources, Marine Resources); voted NO on HR2 Secure the Border Act 2023. CROSS-PARTY DEVIATION: famous Nov 2020 caucus-call quote 'No one should say defund the police ever again'; co-led bipartisan Invest to Protect Act for police funding (PolitiFact rated false the GOP claim she backs defunding); Iron Dome funding YES; bipartisan Social Security Fairness Act signed into law.

0.note: PP-Action: 'Proven Champion for Reproductive Freedom.' Spanberger quote: 'Women in Virginia deserve the freedom to make their own reproductive healthcare decisions without politicians dictating their choices.'

1.note: Cosponsored + voted YES (twice) on Women's Health Protection Act (HR3755, 2021 & 2022); signed SB449 Feb 6 2026 sending VA reproductive-freedom amendment to Nov 2026 ballot.

4.note: Endorsed and funded by Planned Parenthood Advocates of Virginia and EMILYs List in 2025 governor race.

0.note: Voted YES on Respect for Marriage Act 2022 (Roll Calls 373 + 513); as Governor signed HB612 placing marriage-equality constitutional amendment on Nov 2026 ballot.

1.note: Same RFMA votes codify federal recognition of same-sex marriage.

2.note: Original cosponsor of Equality Act (federal SOGI law); on the campaign trail defended LGBTQ youth access and criticized Youngkin trans-policy as 'outing kids.'

4.note: HRC PAC endorsement 2025; Equality Act co-introducer (federalizes LGBTQ promotion in schools/employment/military/public accommodation).

0.note: Campaign platform: pledged to 'oppose efforts that take public dollars out of public schools' — opposes school-choice / voucher / ESA.

1.note: Publicly opposed parental-notification on student gender identity, calling Youngkin policy one that would 'out kids.'

2.note: Quote: 'time to get culture wars out of our schools and let teachers teach.' Equality Act cosponsorship federalizes SOGI curricula in K-12.

4.note: Equality Act explicitly preempts RFRA conscience defenses for faith-based adoption / foster agencies; opposes faith-based placement carve-outs.

1.note: Original cosponsor + YES on Equality Act (HR5), which explicitly preempts RFRA conscience defenses for Christian medical professionals, business owners, adoption agencies, and educators.

2.note: YES on Inflation Reduction Act (HR5376) + YES on Build Back Better Act — both large deficit-spending packages. Heritage Action 5%. No record supporting a balanced-budget amendment.

1.note: Cosponsored + YES on For the People Act (HR1) which preempts state photo voter-ID laws; as Governor opposes SAVE Act proof-of-citizenship requirements.

2.note: HR1 cosponsorship: mandates nationwide early voting and no-excuse mail-in — contrary to single-day in-person.

3.note: HR1 + HR4 cosponsorship: protects mass mail-in voting + ballot drop boxes.

0.note: CROSS-PARTY POSITION: famous Nov 2020 caucus-call rebuke 'No one should say defund the police ever again'; co-led bipartisan Invest to Protect Act funding police pay/hiring; PolitiFact rated 'False' the claim she backs defunding.
Voted NO on HR2 Secure the Border Act 2023 (party-line 219-213) — opposes completed border-barrier + military border presence.

1.note: House issue page commits to an 'earned pathway to legal status' — opposes mandatory deportation.

2.note: Executive Directive 1 (Feb 4 2026) terminated all 287(g) agreements between ICE and VA State Police, DOC, Wildlife Resources, and Marine Resources Commission — actively obstructs federal immigration cooperation.

1.note: Cosponsored + YES on federal Assault Weapons Ban 2022 (HR1808, Roll Call 410); as Governor signed VA SB749/HB217 assault-weapons + high-capacity-mag ban May 14-15 2026 (NRA + VCDL filed suit).

profile.religion: Muslim — sworn in on family Qur'an + 1799 US Constitution Jan 17, 2026; first Muslim woman elected statewide in US history
profile.background: 43rd Lt. Governor of Virginia, sworn Jan 17, 2026 by Chief Justice Cleo Powell. PhD English (Emory). ~30 years at J. Sargeant Reynolds Community College, founding director of Center for Excellence in Teaching & Learning. Prior office: VA State Senate SD-15 (2020–Jan 2026); chaired Senate Education & Health Committee 2024–2026.

ghazala-hashmi.notes_set: Lt. Governor of Virginia (D), sworn Jan 17, 2026; first Muslim woman elected statewide in US. Prior: VA Senate SD-15 (Richmond/Chesterfield) 2020-2026, Education & Health Chair. [2026-06 overnight refinement, state tier] Solidly progressive across the rubric. Patron of SJ247 (2025 reproductive-freedom amendment) AND SJ249 (2025 same-sex-marriage amendment). Sponsored SB280 (2024 'Death with Dignity' physician-assisted suicide; passed Senate 21-19) and SB1098 (2025 shield law for out-of-state abortion + gender-medicine providers). As Ed/Health Chair, panel killed 2023 parental-rights bills on school books and student gender-identity notification, and rejected charter-school expansion. Voted for Virginia Values Act 2020 (state SOGI nondiscrimination, no conscience carve-out). Patron of SB5035 (2020 spec session) civilian police-oversight boards with binding disciplinary authority including termination. Sponsored 'Cover All Kids' Medicaid-like coverage for undocumented children. Voted YES on SB1 (2025) semi-auto firearms + high-capacity-mag ban (Youngkin-vetoed). Endorsed by Planned Parenthood, Reproductive Freedom for All, ReproRising VA PAC, Giffords, Everytown ('gun sense champion'), Brady, VA LGBT+ Democrats. June 2026 (as LG) publicly attacked Commonwealth's Attorneys who declined to enforce the new assault-weapons ban. No cross-party deviations found.

0.note: Patron of SJ247 (2025 constitutional amendment enshrining 'fundamental right to reproductive freedom').

1.note: Patron SJ247 + sponsor SB1098 shield law for out-of-state abortion providers.

3.note: Sponsored SB280 (2024) 'Death with Dignity' physician-assisted suicide; passed Senate 21-19.

4.note: Endorsed by Planned Parenthood Advocates of VA, Reproductive Freedom for All (formerly NARAL), Roe Your Vote VA, Vote Pro Choice.

0.note: Patron of SJ249 (2025 constitutional amendment to repeal VA's same-sex marriage ban and create affirmative right to marry).

1.note: Same SJ249 patronage.

2.note: Sponsored SB1098 shield law for out-of-state pediatric gender-medicine providers.

4.note: Voted YES on Virginia Values Act (2020) — sweeping LGBTQ anti-discrimination law; appointed Equality VA ED Narissa Rahaman to LG transition team.

0.note: 'Strongly opposed to school vouchers'; as Ed/Health Chair, panel rejected charter-school expansion.

1.note: As Ed/Health Chair killed bills requiring schools to notify parents of student 'gender incongruence'; defended kills publicly.

2.note: Chairs panel that rejected bills banning 'inherently divisive teaching concepts' (CRT-style).

1.note: Voted YES on Virginia Values Act (2020) without religious-conscience carve-out for Christian medical professionals, business owners, adoption agencies, or educators.

2.note: Campaign issues page: supports 'expanding early voting, mail-in ballots, and no-excuse absentee voting' — opposed to single-day in-person.

3.note: Same: supports expanding mail-in and absentee voting — supports mass mail-in voting.

0.note: Patron SB5035 (2020 spec session) civilian police-oversight boards with binding disciplinary authority including termination of officers. June 2026 publicly attacked Commonwealth's Attorneys who declined to enforce the new AWB law.

2.note: As LG endorsed Spanberger's Executive Directive 1 ending VA 287(g)/ICE cooperation; presided over Senate during Community Trust Agenda passage restricting local 287(g).

1.note: Voted YES on SB1 (2025) semi-auto firearms + high-capacity-magazine ban; Giffords + Everytown + Brady endorsements.

profile.religion: Roman Catholic — Basilica of St. Mary of the Immaculate Conception, Norfolk
profile.background: 49th Attorney General of Virginia, sworn Jan 17, 2026 by his mother (Norfolk Juvenile & Domestic Relations Chief Judge Lyn M. Simmons); first Black AG of Virginia. Prior office: VA House of Delegates HD-89 (Norfolk), Jan 10, 2018 – Jan 2, 2022. Self-confirmed past member of 'Virginia Planned Parenthood board.' Beat incumbent R Jason Miyares 53.14%-46.45% Nov 4, 2025.

jay-jones.notes_set: Attorney General of Virginia (D), sworn Jan 17, 2026 (49th AG, first Black AG); won 2025 race 53-46 vs R incumbent Miyares. Prior: VA House Delegate HD-89 (Norfolk) 2018-2022. [2026-06 overnight refinement, state tier] Reliably progressive record on the marquee cells: self-confirmed past board member of 'Virginia Planned Parenthood'; co-patron 2019 'Repeal Act' (HB2491) removing ultrasound + 24-hour-wait abortion restrictions; PP Advocates VA + Reproductive Freedom For All + ReproRising VA PAC + Roe Your Vote endorsements; Aug 2025 hosted 'Reproductive Freedom Roundtable' with PP. Voted YES on Virginia Values Act 2020 (LGBTQ nondiscrimination, no conscience carve-out); VA LGBT+ Democrats endorsement. Co-sponsored/voted YES on 2020 Dem omnibus gun package; Everytown $200K + endorsement (later scrubbed after texts surfaced); Giffords + Brady + Prevent Gun Tragedies PAC endorsements; as AG-elect Dec 2025 intervened to salvage VA's universal background-check law on appeal. Anti-sanctuary-ban: 2018 voted NO on HB1257 (would have banned sanctuary jurisdictions); Day-1 as AG announced review of Miyares opinion authorizing ICE detainers. CRITICAL EVIDENCE — 2022 text scandal: Aug 2022 text exchange with R Del. Carrie Coyner surfaced October 2025 (National Review): Jones wrote of then-Speaker Todd Gilbert 'Three people, two bullets / Gilbert, hitler, and pol pot / Gilbert gets two bullets to the head' and that Gilbert's children were 'breeding little fascists' he hoped would die because 'Only when people feel pain personally do they move on policy,' and that he'd 'go to their funerals to piss on their graves.' Coyner replied: 'Please stop … It really bothers me when you talk about hurting people or wishing death on them.' Jones publicly apologized ('reading back those words made me sick to my stomach'). Coyner separately alleges a 2020 phone call where Jones said about police 'Well, maybe if a few of them died, that they would move on, not shooting people, not killing people' — contested by Jones. Spanberger called the comments 'disgusting.' Jones was co-patron of 2020 qualified-immunity ban with floor quote 'take a whole bunch of frivolous lawsuits if it means that someone who is shot 7 times in the back gets a day in court.'

0.note: Self-confirmed past member of 'Virginia Planned Parenthood board'; co-patron 2019 Repeal Act (HB2491) removing ultrasound + 24-hour-wait abortion restrictions.

1.note: Co-patron of the abortion-deregulation Repeal Act; hosted PP Reproductive Freedom Roundtable Aug 2025.

4.note: Endorsed by Planned Parenthood Advocates of Virginia, Reproductive Freedom For All (formerly NARAL), ReproRising VA PAC, Roe Your Vote Virginia.

0.note: Voted YES on Virginia Values Act (SB868/HB1663, 2020) — state LGBTQ anti-discrimination law; endorsed by Virginia LGBT+ Democrats.

1.note: Same VVA YES vote codifies sexual-orientation protections including same-sex couples in public-accommodation law.

2.note: VVA YES vote includes 'gender identity' as a protected class in employment / housing / public accommodation.

4.note: Virginia LGBT+ Democrats endorsement signals affirmative alignment with LGBTQ promotion in public policy.

1.note: YES on Virginia Values Act 2020 — no religious-conscience carve-out for Christian medical professionals, business owners, adoption agencies, or educators.

0.note: Co-patron 2020 qualified-immunity ban; floor quote 'take a whole bunch of frivolous lawsuits if it means that someone who is shot 7 times in the back gets a day in court'; Coyner alleges 2020 phone-call statement that 'a few [police] died' would change policy (Jones denies). Not affirmative police backing.

4.note: August 2022 text exchange w/ Del. Coyner: Jones wrote of then-Speaker Gilbert 'two bullets to the head,' that Gilbert's children were 'breeding little fascists' he hoped would die, and that he'd 'go to their funerals to piss on their graves' — antithetical to a magistrate's God-ordained duty under Rom 13:4 to bear the sword to restrain evil. Jones publicly apologized.

2.note: Day-1 as AG announced review of Miyares opinion that authorized VA jails honoring ICE detainers; voted NO on 2018 HB1257 (would have banned sanctuary jurisdictions, per Restoration News tracking).

1.note: Self-claim: 'helped expand background checks…pass a landmark red flag law.' Everytown $200K donation + endorsement (later scrubbed after texts); Giffords + Brady + Prevent Gun Tragedies PAC endorsements; as AG-elect Dec 2025 intervened to salvage VA's universal background-check law on appeal.
